hold_reason: Timestamp-safe public index addition/deletion announcement event source was unavailable/incomplete; the mandatory >=150 surviving liquid tradable common-stock event gate could not be verified without fabricating event data.
unblock_condition: Provide or implement a reproducible point-in-time/public-availability source for S&P 500, Nasdaq-100 and/or Russell large-cap common-stock additions/deletions with announcement timestamp or conservative lag, add/delete side, index family, identifier mapping, exclusion flags, and >=150 liquid tradable Alpaca/qfa-covered events after filters.

event_source_gate.reason: No complete timestamp-safe historical event source was available in the clean worktree or proven by lightweight public endpoint checks.

source_options_checked.evidence: Content/name searches found no reusable timestamp-safe index-addition/deletion source table; no raw source files retained.
HTTP 403 Forbidden during lightweight source-gate check.
HTTP 200 HTML page; checked content did not establish a historical timestamp-safe Nasdaq-100 add/delete announcement feed.
HTTP 200 HTML page for one URL and HTTP 404 for a checked resources URL; no complete timestamp-safe historical add/delete feed established.
No credentials, licensed dump, or PIT archive was available or used.

controls.reason: No accepted event set or return stream exists.

decision_rationale: Hold, not performance rejection. AR-141's core data source is an external point-in-time announcement dependency. Running a qfa/Alpaca backtest now would require inferred calendar or OHLCV proxies and would violate the issue's real-data/timestamp-safe constraints.
